mapping.a_element: linear sigma model moduli spaces realized as Quot/hyperquot schemes
mapping.b_element: moduli space of solutions to the perturbed Witten equation

mapping.a_element: transitions between (non-birational) Kahler phases across walls in the GLSM Kahler moduli
mapping.b_element: wall-crossing of the deformed virtual cycle under perturbation matched to Picard-Lefschetz

mapping.a_element: GLSM's connection to enumerative geometry via its geometric (CY) phase
mapping.b_element: extended virtual cycle satisfying axioms similar to Gromov-Witten and r-spin theory

brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is a nonabelian gauged linear sigma model whose Kahler phases (GIT/quotient phase structure, homological projective duality) realize different geometries, whereas paper B's machinery is geometric analysis: constructing a virtual fundamental cycle from the moduli space of solutions to a nonlinear elliptic PDE (the Witten equation), with wall-crossing matched to Picard-Lefschetz. They share the same Landau-Ginzburg/CY mirror-symmetry ecosystem and vocabulary (moduli spaces, wall-crossing, Gromov-Witten), but the governing constructions differ, so no role-for-role mapping of the same machinery holds.
